title: Promenade Beach
tagline: Promenade Beach — beach town of Puducherry

overview.short: Rock Beach (or, known as, "Promenade Beach", French: Plage de la Promenade) is the popular stretch of beachfront in the city of Puducherry, India, along the Bay of Bengal.
overview.description: Rock Beach (or, known as, "Promenade Beach", French: Plage de la Promenade) is the popular stretch of beachfront in the city of Puducherry, India, along the Bay of Bengal. It is a 1.2-kilometre-long stretch in Pondicherry, starts from War Memorial and end at Dupleix Park on the Goubert Avenue.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: French War Memorial (Puducherry)
topPlaces.description: The French War Memorial in Puducherry, India (French: Monument aux Combattants des Indes Françaises morts pour la Patrie) is a war memorial dedicated to residents of French India who died for the country during the First World War. It is on Goubert Avenue in Puducherry, located opposite to the Gandhi statue.

topPlaces.name: Puducherry taluk
topPlaces.description: Puducherry taluk (Tamil: [puðutːʃeːɾi]) is one of four taluks in the Puducherry district of the union territory of Puducherry. It comprises the city of Pondicherry and villages of Ariyankuppam Commune.

topPlaces.name: Aayi Mandapam
topPlaces.description: Aayi Mandapam, also known as Park Monument (French: Monument du Parc), is a white monument in Pondicherry, India, built during the time of Napoleon III, Emperor of France. It is situated in the center of Bharati Park.

topPlaces.name: Siege of Pondicherry (1778)
topPlaces.description: The siege of Pondicherry was the first military action on the Indian subcontinent following the declaration of war between Great Britain and France in the American Revolutionary War. A British force besieged the French Indian port of Pondicherry in August 1778, which capitulated after ten weeks of siege.

topPlaces.name: Battle of Pondicherry
topPlaces.description: The Battle of Pondicherry was a naval battle between a British squadron under Vice-Admiral George Pocock and French squadron under Comte d'Aché on 10 September 1759 off the Carnatic coast of India near Pondicherry during the Seven Years' War. Pocock attempted to intercept d'Aché, whose squadron was carrying reinforcements and money for the French forces in Pondicherry.

topPlaces.name: Lok Niwas, Pondicherry
topPlaces.description: Lok Niwas, formerly Raj Niwas (translation: Government House) is the official residence of the Lieutenant Governor of Puducherry. It is located in Puducherry's capital city of Pondicherry.

topPlaces.name: Our Lady of Angels Church, Puducherry
topPlaces.description: Our Lady of Angels Church (also called Église Notre-Dame des Anges, White Chapel, Kaps Koil) is the fourth oldest church in Puducherry, a Union territory in South India. The original structure was built in Greco Roman architecture by Napoleon III in 1855, with the architect being Louis Guerre.

image.alt: Our Lady of Angels Church, Puducherry

topPlaces.name: Fort Louis (Pondicherry)
topPlaces.description: Fort Louis or Fort Saint Louis was a French fort that stood in Pondicherry on the eastern coast of India. The fort was built around 1701 by François Martin and completed posthumously around 1706.

items.note: The French War Memorial in Puducherry, India (French: Monument aux Combattants des Indes Françaises morts pour la Patrie) is a war memorial dedicated to residents of French India who died for the country during the First World War. It is on Goubert Avenue in Puducherry, located opposite to the Gandhi statue.

Puducherry taluk (Tamil: [puðutːʃeːɾi]) is one of four taluks in the Puducherry district of the union territory of Puducherry. It comprises the city of Pondicherry and villages of Ariyankuppam Commune.

Aayi Mandapam, also known as Park Monument (French: Monument du Parc), is a white monument in Pondicherry, India, built during the time of Napoleon III, Emperor of France. It is situated in the center of Bharati Park.

The siege of Pondicherry was the first military action on the Indian subcontinent following the declaration of war between Great Britain and France in the American Revolutionary War. A British force besieged the French Indian port of Pondicherry in August 1778, which capitulated after ten weeks of siege.

The Battle of Pondicherry was a naval battle between a British squadron under Vice-Admiral George Pocock and French squadron under Comte d'Aché on 10 September 1759 off the Carnatic coast of India near Pondicherry during the Seven Years' War. Pocock attempted to intercept d'Aché, whose squadron was carrying reinforcements and money for the French forces in Pondicherry.
Lok Niwas, formerly Raj Niwas (translation: Government House) is the official residence of the Lieutenant Governor of Puducherry. It is located in Puducherry's capital city of Pondicherry.

items.activity: Our Lady of Angels Church, Puducherry
items.note: Our Lady of Angels Church (also called Église Notre-Dame des Anges, White Chapel, Kaps Koil) is the fourth oldest church in Puducherry, a Union territory in South India. The original structure was built in Greco Roman architecture by Napoleon III in 1855, with the architect being Louis Guerre.
Fort Louis or Fort Saint Louis was a French fort that stood in Pondicherry on the eastern coast of India. The fort was built around 1701 by François Martin and completed posthumously around 1706.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Promenade Beach?
faq.a: The best time to visit Promenade Beach is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Promenade Beach?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Chennai International Airport (124 km) and the nearest railway station is Chennai Central (137 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Promenade Beach?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Promenade Beach at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Promenade Beach?
faq.a: Stays in Promenade Beach start from ₹1,200 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Promenade Beach?
faq.a: The top attractions are French War Memorial (Puducherry), Puducherry taluk, Aayi Mandapam, Siege of Pondicherry (1778), Battle of Pondicherry.
